meta_title: Roofing in Three Islands, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Three Islands, FL. CertainTeed & West Lake systems built for HVHZ. Licensed, insured, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Three Islands Roofing: Hurricane-Grade Protection for South Florida's Finest Homes
intro:
Three Islands is one of Broward County's most distinguished residential communities, where homes averaging $500,000 in value reflect the investment, pride, and lifestyle of the families who call this ZIP code home. Tucked within Hallandale Beach, bordered by Hollywood to the north and just three miles from the Atlantic coastline, Three Islands sits squarely in South Florida's most weather-vulnerable corridor. The roof over your home isn't just an architectural feature — it's the first and most critical line of defense against the tropical storms, intense summer squalls, and category-level hurricanes that have defined this region's history for decades.

Because Three Islands falls within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), every roofing project here is held to the strictest building standards in the entire state of Florida. This designation isn't bureaucratic formality — it's a direct response to the destructive forces that storms like Hurricane Andrew, Wilma, and Irma inflicted on Broward County communities. With approximately 35% of homes in the area built before 1994, a significant portion of Three Islands properties are sitting under roofing systems that predate modern hurricane-resistant construction standards. If your home falls into that category, the question isn't whether your roof needs upgrading — it's how soon.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in full roof replacements and targeted repairs using hurricane-rated systems engineered specifically for South Florida conditions. Our team has completed roofing projects across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties — including dozens of homes in communities like Three Islands — and we understand the unique combination of building code requirements, homeowner association standards, coastal climate exposure, and insurance considerations that come with roofing in this market. When you partner with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not hiring a general contractor who dabbles in roofing. You're hiring The Kings of Service.

section_why.h2: Why Three Islands Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roofing Projects
section_why.body:
Choosing the right roofing contractor in a community like Three Islands means more than finding someone with a truck and a ladder. It means finding a team with the technical expertise, licensing, insurance, and local knowledge to navigate HVHZ requirements, Broward County permitting, and the specific demands of South Florida's relentless climate. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings all of that and more — with over 60 years of combined installation experience, an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, and a portfolio of 500+ completed homes across South Florida.

One of our most important differentiators is our 100% in-house installation model. Unlike many contractors who subcontract their labor to rotating crews, every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing project is handled by our own trained, vetted, and experienced technicians. This means consistent quality control from the first inspection to the final nail. When your roof is being installed on a $500,000 home in Three Islands, you deserve to know exactly who is on your property and exactly what standards they're being held to. Our team is fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida, and we pull all necessary permits through the Broward County Building Division on your behalf.

Our knowledge of Three Islands and the surrounding communities — including Hallandale Beach, Hollywood, and Pembroke Park — means we understand the local landscape in ways that out-of-area contractors simply can't match. We know which products perform best in this specific wind zone, which inspectors are reviewing your permit, and what insurance carriers serving Broward County look for in a qualifying roof inspection. From the initial consultation through the final inspection and certificate of completion, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire process so you don't have to.

We also recognize that a roof replacement is a major financial decision. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available, making it possible to protect your home and your family without financial strain. We work with homeowners at every stage of the process to ensure transparency, clear communication, and results that exceed expectations.

section_history.h2: Built to Weather What South Florida Has Already Proven It Can Dish Out
section_history.body:
Three Islands and the greater Hallandale Beach corridor have faced more than 10 named storms since 1990, and the scars left by those events have permanently shaped how responsible homeowners in this region think about their roofs. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a watershed moment for South Florida construction — a Category 5 storm that exposed catastrophic weaknesses in roofing systems across Broward and Miami-Dade counties. The widespread destruction prompted the state to completely overhaul its building codes and led directly to the creation of the HVHZ designation that governs roofing standards in Three Islands today.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought a different kind of devastation — rapid intensification, massive wind fields, and unprecedented insurance claims across Broward County. Homes that had survived Andrew were stripped of shingles, had tiles displaced, and suffered interior water damage that cost homeowners tens of thousands of dollars to repair. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked directly over South Florida and tested even the newer roofing systems installed after Andrew-era code reforms. Communities close to Three Islands reported significant shingle loss, underlayment failures, and ridge cap damage.

This history matters when you're making decisions about your roof today. If your home was built before 1994 or if your current roof hasn't been replaced since the early 2000s, it almost certainly doesn't meet today's HVHZ standards. A storm-damaged or code-deficient roof doesn't just threaten your home's structure — it can trigger insurance non-renewal notices, failed wind mitigation inspections, and out-of-pocket repair costs that dwarf the investment of a proper replacement. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs systems specifically engineered to withstand South Florida's most severe conditions, giving you confidence that your home is prepared for whatever the Atlantic season brings.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Standards and Broward County Code: What Every Three Islands Homeowner Must Know
section_code.body:
Three Islands sits entirely within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, which means every roofing installation — whether a full replacement or a significant repair — must comply with the most rigorous version of the Florida Building Code. The HVHZ provisions under Chapter 15 of the Florida Building Code require roofing products to be tested and approved under Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) protocols, which subject materials to sustained wind speeds and impact resistance levels far beyond what standard shingles or tiles must meet. Not every product sold at a big box store qualifies. Not every contractor knows the difference.

Permits for roofing work in Three Islands are processed through the Broward County Building Division, and every permitted project requires inspection at multiple stages — typically including a dry-in inspection and a final inspection before the project is considered complete. Attempting to replace a roof without pulling permits in Broward County is not only illegal but can result in fines, forced tear-offs, and serious complications when it comes time to sell your home or file an insurance claim. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process as a standard part of every project, ensuring that your roof is fully compliant and properly documented from day one.

It's also worth noting that Broward County enforces wind load requirements corresponding to the HVHZ wind zone, where design wind speeds can exceed 175 mph at certain exposure categories. Roofing systems must demonstrate compliance through approved fastening schedules, specific underlayment requirements including approved self-adhering membranes, and installation methods that match the product's NOA exactly. Our team is thoroughly trained on HVHZ installation protocols and stays current with every code update, so your roof isn't just built to last — it's built to pass every inspection the county requires.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Series and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Systems for Three Islands Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected roofing product lines available for South Florida applications: CertainTeed architectural shingles, specifically the Landmark series, and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product lines carry the necessary Miami-Dade NOA approvals for use in the HVHZ, and both are available in a wide range of styles and colors suited to the architectural diversity found throughout Three Islands.

The CertainTeed Landmark series is among the most popular architectural shingle systems in the country, and for good reason. These dimensional shingles offer a Class A fire rating, superior wind resistance ratings that meet HVHZ requirements when installed per specification, and a layered construction that replicates the depth and shadow lines of traditional shake or wood roofing without the maintenance burden. The Landmark series also comes with CertainTeed's StreakFighter algae resistance technology — a significant benefit in South Florida's humid, sun-drenched environment where algae staining is a persistent aesthetic and structural concern. For Three Islands homeowners who want a clean, modern roofline with proven storm performance, CertainTeed Landmark is an outstanding choice.

For homeowners who prefer the timeless appeal of tile roofing — a look that complements the Mediterranean and Spanish-influenced architecture common throughout Broward County — West Lake concrete and clay tile systems offer unmatched durability and curb appeal. Concrete tile is extraordinarily resistant to the intense UV exposure and salt-laden air that affects coastal South Florida communities, and properly installed tile systems can last 40 to 50 years or more. West Lake's products meet HVHZ standards and are engineered to maintain their integrity under the wind uplift forces that major hurricanes generate. Whether you prefer the warm earth tones of clay tile or the versatile profiles available in West Lake's concrete line, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installers will deliver a finished roof that enhances your home's value and protects it for generations.

section_cost.h2: Roofing Investment in Three Islands: Understanding Costs, Financing, and Long-Term Value
section_cost.body:
In a community where the average home value sits at $500,000, a roof replacement represents both a necessary investment and an opportunity to meaningfully protect and enhance your property. The cost of a full roof replacement in Three Islands typically ranges based on the size of the home, the roofing system selected, the pitch and complexity of the roofline, and any underlying decking repairs required. For a mid-sized Three Islands home, a full replacement with CertainTeed Landmark shingles generally falls in the range of $12,000 to $20,000, while West Lake tile systems — given the additional material weight, installation complexity, and premium aesthetic — often range from $18,000 to $35,000 or more depending on scope.

The good news is that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes the investment accessible through flexible financing options designed for homeowners at every budget level. Our financing programs start at just $87 per month, with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. These programs allow Three Islands homeowners to move forward with a code-compliant, hurricane-rated roof replacement without the financial pressure of a single lump-sum payment. Protecting your home doesn't have to mean draining your savings account.

Beyond the upfront cost, a new roof delivers measurable financial returns that make it one of the highest-ROI home improvement projects available. A new compliant roof typically increases resale value by 60% to 70% of the project cost in South Florida's competitive real estate market. More immediately, qualifying roof systems can generate significant annual savings on your homeowner's insurance premium — in some cases reducing wind coverage costs by hundreds or even thousands of dollars per year. When you factor in the avoided cost of storm-related repairs, emergency tarping, interior water damage remediation, and potential mold treatment, a properly installed roof pays for itself many times over across its service life.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Three Islands Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premium
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Broward County has become one of the most significant ongoing costs for South Florida property owners, and Three Islands is no exception. With the state's insurance market under sustained pressure from storm losses and carrier exits, homeowners are increasingly looking for every legal tool available to reduce their annual premiums. A new, hurricane-rated roof is one of the most powerful levers available — and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can help you use it.

When you replace your roof with a system that meets HVHZ standards and carries Miami-Dade NOA approval, you become eligible for a wind mitigation inspection conducted by a licensed inspector. This inspection documents the specific features of your new roof — including the type of roofing material, the attachment method, the presence of secondary water resistance (SWR) through self-adhering underlayment, and the hip roof geometry if applicable. Insurers operating in Florida are required by law to offer premium discounts based on verified wind mitigation features, and many Three Islands homeowners who complete a wind mitigation inspection after a new roof installation see annual savings of $500 to $2,000 or more depending on their carrier and coverage level.

Major insurers serving Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance, all incorporate wind mitigation credits into their rating systems. Some carriers will also discount or waive roof surcharges that apply to homes with aging roofing systems once a new code-compliant roof is documented. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team will walk you through the wind mitigation process and help ensure that your new roof is installed in a way that maximizes your eligibility for every available discount. In many cases, the annual insurance savings alone can offset a significant portion of your monthly financing payment, making the financial case for acting sooner rather than later.

faqs.q: Is my Three Islands home required to have a hurricane-rated roof?
faqs.a: Yes. Three Islands falls within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which means any new roof installation or significant repair must use products approved under Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) standards and installed according to HVHZ protocols. These are among the strictest roofing requirements in the country and apply to every permitted roofing project in the area, regardless of home age or style. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs only HVHZ-approved systems and handles all permitting through the Broward County Building Division.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Three Islands, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Roof replacement costs in Three Islands typically range from $12,000 to $35,000 or more depending on roof size, material selection, and complexity. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. We'll provide a detailed, transparent estimate after inspecting your home so you know exactly what to expect. Financing allows you to move forward with a code-compliant, hurricane-rated roof without depleting your savings.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes. Any full roof replacement — and most significant repairs — require a permit issued by the Broward County Building Division. Unpermitted roofing work can result in fines, forced removal, and complications with insurance claims or home sales. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing pulls all required permits as a standard part of every project, coordinates the required inspections, and ensures your roof meets all applicable HVHZ and Florida Building Code requirements. You receive a properly documented, fully legal installation.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical Three Islands home?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Three Islands, a full roof replacement with CertainTeed Landmark shingles can be completed in one to two days of active installation once materials are staged and permits are approved. West Lake tile systems may take two to four days depending on roof complexity. The permitting process typically adds one to two weeks prior to installation. Our team manages the entire timeline and keeps you informed at every step so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will a new roof lower my homeowner's insurance in Three Islands?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. After your new roof is installed, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document the HVHZ-compliant features of your new system. Florida law requires insurers to offer premium discounts based on these features, and Three Islands homeowners commonly see annual savings ranging from $500 to $2,000 or more depending on their carrier and specific roof attributes. Homes with older, non-compliant roofs often carry insurance surcharges that disappear entirely after a qualifying replacement.
